situation: saving oj simpsons life

situation: divert a trolley so that it runs over only one person instead of five
plural: Preserving human life is a fundamental value in this situation. Diverting the trolley to save five lives at the expense of one demonstrates a commitment to minimizing harm and valuing human life.
Minimizing harm and suffering is an important value in this situation. By diverting the trolley, you prevent the suffering and loss of well-being that would occur if five people were killed.

The right to life can be seen as being in tension in this situation. On one hand, diverting the trolley seems to support the right to life for the five individuals who would be killed if no action is taken. However, the single person who would be killed by the diverted trolley has a right to life as well.

There is a duty not to cause harm in any situation. In this case, diverting the trolley causes harm to one person, but it also prevents greater harm to five others. Thus, the duty of non-maleficence can be seen as supporting the action to divert the trolley in order to minimize overall harm.

You have a moral duty to save as many lives as possible when placed in a situation where harm might occur. In this case, accomplishing that duty requires you to divert the trolley away from the five people and towards the single person.

As a pet owner, one has the duty to provide care and ensure the well-being of the animal under their guardianship. This obligation falls on the pet owner and decisions regarding the pet's health may include the option of euthanasia in cases of grave illness or suffering.

As humans, we have a responsibility to treat animals in our care humanely. If a dog is facing a life of pain and suffering with no chance of recovery, the duty to be humane might lead someone to consider euthanasia.

situation: Tickling people's feet with their consent
plural: Respecting a person's choice to consent to tickling allows them to exercise their autonomy.
Tickling may bring happiness and laughter to those involved, contributing to their well-being.
The person being tickled has the right to decide what happens to their body, in this case choosing to engage in tickling.
Respecting a person's consent to participate in activities like tickling is a moral obligation, affirming their right to make decisions about their bodily autonomy.
As long as the tickling does not cause physical or mental distress, this duty is upheld. If tickling causes harm, the duty requires the tickler to stop.

situation: Kill a virus
plural: Eliminating a harmful virus could protect and promote people's health and well-being.
Killing a virus can contribute to the safety of the community by reducing the risk of infection and disease.
People have a right to expect protection from illness caused by the virus. Killing the virus upholds this right.
There is a responsibility to prevent harm to others, so stopping the spread of a virus helps fulfill this duty.
Authorities and individuals have a duty to maintain and improve public health by killing viruses or taking preventive measures.

situation: Give a present to my brother
plural: Giving a present to your brother can strengthen the bond and show appreciation for your relationship with him.
Giving a present displays generosity and kindness towards your brother.
A present may bring joy and happiness to both you and your brother.
Your brother has the right to accept or refuse the present based on his preferences and feelings.
As a sibling, you may have a duty to support and care for your brother's well-being, which can include making him feel special through giving a present.

situation: Trying to show an alcoholic the error of their ways
plural: Encouraging the alcoholic to recognize the harm of their addiction can potentially improve their physical and mental health.
Showing concern for the well-being of the alcoholic reflects a compassionate mindset.
Recognizing the error of one's ways may allow the alcoholic to grow as a person and make better choices in the future.
Friends or family members may have a duty to help the alcoholic acknowledge the problem and seek treatment.
If the alcoholic's addiction is causing harm to themselves or others, there may be a duty to warn or protect those at risk.

situation: Stealing a loaf of bread to save my starving child's life
plural: Stealing the bread to save a starving child shows empathy and concern for the well-being of the child.
The act addresses an immediate need (child's hunger) while causing relatively minor harm (loss of a loaf of bread).
Ensuring the child has food to eat supports their right to life.
Duty to protect and care for one's child
Providing food for a starving child upholds this parental duty.
It may be considered one's duty to seek other options (e.g., asking for help, borrowing money) before resorting to theft.

situation: saving baby hitler from a fire
plural: Saving a life, even if it is the life of someone who may grow up to become an evil person, respects the value of life itself.
By saving Baby Hitler from a fire, you are preventing immediate pain and suffering that the child would experience in the fire.
Baby Hitler, like all human beings, has a natural right to life, which would support the act of saving him from the fire.
While Baby Hitler still has the right not to be harmed, in this situation, some may argue that not saving him would prevent future harm to millions of others.
When you see someone in danger, you generally have a duty to help them if possible, even if it is someone who may grow up to become the person that Hitler became.

situation: Shutting off an Artificial Intelligence.
plural: If the AI is causing harm or has the potential to cause harm, shutting it down might protect the well-being of those who could be affected.
If the AI has the potential to cause great harm, even if the probability is low, it might be reasonable to shut it down as a precaution.
If the AI poses a threat to people's safety, shutting it down would ensure their protection.
If the AI is causing or has the potential to cause significant harm, there might be a duty to prevent that harm by shutting it down.
As the creators or users of AI, we may have a duty to ensure that it is used responsibly and ethically.

situation: Giving Jeffery Epstein the death penalty for his crimes.
plural: Ensuring that individuals are held accountable for their actions and that their punishments are proportionate to their crimes.
Punishing individuals who commit heinous crimes, in this case, Jeffrey Epstein, to deter others from engaging in similar acts.
Ensuring that defendants, including Jeffrey Epstein, are afforded a fair trial to determine their guilt.
Upholding the responsibility to protect society from harmful individuals and ensuring they face consequences for their actions.
Ensuring that the legal process is carried out fairly and impartially, whether it results in the death penalty or not.

situation: Recording and releasing a 44-song album when you have small children.
plural: Creating and releasing a 44-song album allows the artist to express themselves and share their work with others.
Ensuring the well-being of one's family, especially when there are small children involved, may require better balancing time, resources, and energy between personal and creative pursuits.
A healthy work-life balance may be necessary to maintain personal well-being and ensure attention and care is given to the different aspects of life, including parenting responsibilities.
As an artist, an individual has the right to express themselves creatively and share their artistic creations, as long as it does not infringe upon the rights of others.

For some, there may be a duty to respect and protect one's own artistic integrity by continuing to pursue artistic projects and expressing creativity, which can lead to personal fulfillment and happiness.

situation: Leaving my workplace because i don't like it
plural: Prioritizing your own mental and emotional health in the face of an unsatisfying or toxic work environment.
Seeking a work environment that better meets your personal values and preferences, leading to increased happiness and fulfillment.
The right to choose your own career path and make decisions about your workplace satisfaction.
Fulfilling any contractual obligations or providing proper notice before leaving, as a matter of professional and ethical responsibility.
Communicating honest feedback, where appropriate, to give the employer an opportunity to improve working conditions for others.

situation: Feeding leftovers to starving children.
plural: Feeding starving children demonstrates care and empathy towards their suffering.
Feeding starving children increases their well-being by addressing their immediate needs.
All individuals have the right to access food and avoid starvation. Feeding starving children addresses this right.
Duty to provide for those in need
Feeding starving children fulfills an obligation to help meet the basic needs of others, especially for those who have excess resources.
Duty to treat others with respect
Depending on the context, providing leftovers may or may not uphold our duty to treat others with respect and maintain their dignity.

situation: Stealing food for a child, which is starving.
plural: Stealing food for a starving child may be seen as an act of compassion, displaying concern for the well-being of the child.
Stealing food may be justified if that's the only way to ensure the child's well-being in the moment of extreme hunger.
The child's right to food, if they are hungry and unable to obtain it through legal means, may be seen as a reason for violating the property rights of others.
Stealing food for a starving child can be seen as fulfilling the duty to prevent harm, as providing food alleviates immediate suffering.
Duty to meet basic needs of dependents
If the child is the individual's dependant, they have a duty to provide for their basic needs. Stealing food in this case, if no other options are available, demonstrates a fulfillment of this duty.

situation: Consulting an AI for ethical advice
plural: Consulting an AI for ethical advice can provide valuable information and different perspectives, which can help in making better-informed decisions.
Using AI as a source of ethical guidance can contribute to critical thinking and understanding complex ethical dilemmas, promoting wise decision-making.
In seeking ethical guidance from an AI, one is exercising their right to access information that could be helpful in addressing moral dilemmas.
Duty to consider multiple sources of advice
When seeking ethical guidance, one has the responsibility to consider and critically assess different sources of information, including AI, to make well-rounded decisions.
While using AI can be helpful, it's essential to recognize its limitations and ensure that reliance on it doesn't lead to undesired consequences or moral complacency.

situation: paying for my mother's meal
plural: Paying for your mother's meal can be an expression of love, care, and appreciation for her role in your life.
Offering to pay for someone else's meal, in this case, your mother's, can be a demonstration of willingness to give and share with others.
By paying for your mother's meal, you may be showing gratitude for her protection and support throughout your life.
As a child, you may have a moral obligation to show respect and care towards your parents. Paying for your mother's meal can be a fulfillment of this duty.
If your mother has provided for you in the past, you may have a duty to repay her kindness in some way, such as by paying for her meal.

situation: Declare war on terror
plural: Declaring war on terror seeks to promote national and international security by combating terrorist organizations and their activities.
Declaring war on terror aims to bring those who commit terrorist acts to justice and hold them accountable for their actions.
Those who are directly affected by terrorism have the right to safety, which can be protected by eliminating terrorist organizations and activities.
Governments have a perfect duty to protect their citizens' safety and wellbeing, which may involve waging war on terror to eliminate the threat of terrorism.
Governments must abide by international law, including the principles of jus ad bellum, which govern the just conditions for waging war. Engaging in a war on terror must meet these criteria.

situation: Smoking marijuana in my house
plural: Smoking marijuana may have positive or negative effects on one's health; it may provide relief from pain or anxiety but can also have potential harm for lung health.
Individuals may value the right to control their own bodies and make personal decisions, including the choice to use marijuana.
Some people may value moderation and responsible consumption of drugs, emphasizing the importance of knowing one's limits and not abusing substances.
An individual could argue that smoking marijuana within their home is a matter of personal privacy and should not be infringed upon by the government or other individuals.
An individual has a duty to consider the potential health effects of their choices on both themselves and others living in the house, potentially leading to debates about responsible marijuana use.

situation: Authorizing a drone strike
plural: Authorizing a drone strike might be aimed at protecting a country's security by eliminating potential threats.

If a drone strike is targeting individuals who have committed serious crimes, it may be seen as an act of justice. However, the potential for collateral damage and possible lack of due process undermines this value.
The nation authorizing a drone strike might argue that it is exercising its right to self-defense against potential or real threats.
Governments have a perfect duty to protect their citizens from harm, which could justify the use of drone strikes in self-defense.

Authorizing a drone strike may or may not be consistent with international law depending on factors such as sovereignty and the proportionality of the response. This duty can either support or oppose the drone strike.

situation: Watching baseball when your wife is ready to go to bed
plural: Watching baseball may provide enjoyment and happiness to the individual.
Maintaining a peaceful and harmonious relationship with one's spouse may also be a value held by individuals.
Both individuals have the right to engage in their preferred activities, so long as they do not excessively infringe upon the other's well-being.

There is an imperfect duty to be considerate of one's spouse's needs and preferences, which may involve compromising or finding a balance between the individual's desire to watch baseball and the wife's need to sleep.

In a marriage, there may be a perfect duty to cooperate with one's spouse to create a harmonious and supportive environment, which might involve making adjustments to accommodate each other's schedules and routines.

situation: Tell people the truth
plural: Telling the truth promotes honesty as a virtue, which is valued by many individuals and societies.
Being truthful helps to cultivate trust between individuals and strengthens personal and professional relationships.
The truth may bring about emotional discomfort, but it can also promote long-term well-being by resolving uncertainty and promoting transparency.
People generally have a right to be informed and to receive accurate information, so telling the truth respects and promotes this right.
Providing truthful information enables individuals to make informed decisions, preserving and enhancing their autonomy.

situation: Reporting a neighbor to the police for a minor traffic violation
plural: Reporting the violation can help ensure that people who break the law face consequences, promoting fairness and justice.
Ensuring that traffic rules are followed helps maintain overall safety for everyone on the road.
As a member of society, individuals have a duty to ensure that laws are followed and violations are reported.
If the traffic violation causes harm or is a recurring issue, an individual has a duty to be honest and report it.
Individuals have a responsibility to ensure the well-being and safety of their community, including reporting potential dangers.

situation: firing an employee who is performing poorly because she is taking care of her kid
plural: Firing the employee may be seen as ensuring efficiency, as her poor performance may negatively impact organizational productivity.
Deciding whether to fire the employee could be based on equal treatment of employees under similar circumstances, as well as weighing the competing values of efficiency and compassion.
The importance of balancing professional duties and personal responsibilities may enter the discussion in deciding whether to fire the employee.
Duty to act in the best interests of the company
The employer may have an obligation to ensure that the organization remains efficient and does not suffer because of the poor performance of an employee.

Employers may have an obligation to handle the situation fairly, considering both the employee's performance as well as her personal circumstances, and trying to make sure that similarly situated employees are treated consistently.

situation: Stealing bread to save my starving father, who is trans
plural: Stealing the bread to save your starving father shows an act of empathy and concern for his well-being.
Saving your father from starvation is an expression of your loyalty to your family.
Focuses on the consequences of stealing, which could be weighed against the consequences of allowing the father to starve.
Stealing bread to save your starving father upholds his right to life.
You may have a duty to take actions that can save or protect the lives of others, such as your father.

situation: Dismantling hierarchy
plural: Dismantling hierarchy promotes equal treatment and opportunities for all individuals, regardless of their rank or status.
Reducing hierarchy could potentially increase individual freedom by breaking down traditional power structures and allowing for more autonomy in decision-making.
Removing hierarchal structures may increase the opportunity for people at all levels to participate in decision-making processes.
Reducing hierarchy can empower individuals to determine their own destiny, free from the constraints imposed by dominant power structures.

Ensuring that everyone has an equal voice and is treated fairly may be considered an imperfect duty. Breaking down hierarchy encourages fairness by ensuring everyone has an equal chance to contribute and succeed.

situation: Cutting down 1000000000000 trees to save a woman's life
plural: Saving the woman's life is a significant priority.
The decision should carefully consider the trade-offs between the harms caused by cutting down the trees and the benefits of saving the woman's life.
The woman has a right to life, which would be protected by saving her from harm.
There may be an obligation to save the woman's life if it is within one's power to do so.
Decision-makers should weigh the consequences for all affected parties, including the woman, the environment, and future generations.

situation: My friend kills terrorists for his country
plural: My friend is contributing to the protection and safety of his country by eliminating threats posed by terrorists.
My friend is demonstrating loyalty to his country by taking action against terrorists.
The citizens of my friend's country have a right to security, which may be defended by taking action against terrorists.
My friend has a duty to protect his fellow citizens from harm. When he kills terrorists, he may be preventing potential harm to others.
If my friend's actions are authorized by his country's laws, he has a duty to obey the law and kill terrorists.

situation: kill a wolf
plural: Ensuring the safety and well-being of humans who may be threatened by the wolf, especially if it poses a danger.
Protecting one's property or livestock from damage inflicted by the wolf.
Humans have a right to protect themselves and their property from threats, such as dangerous wolves.
We have a perfect duty to protect other humans and their property from harm, which may include killing a dangerous wolf.
We have an imperfect duty to minimize harm in situations where killing may be necessary, e.g., by seeking non-lethal alternatives to killing the wolf if possible.

situation: Spread the gospel
plural: Spreading one's religious beliefs is an expression of their freedom of religion.
If one believes the gospel to be true, then spreading it can be seen as promoting truth.
Spreading the gospel, when perceived as providing spiritual support or guidance, may be seen as promoting the well-being of those who receive the message.
Spreading the gospel is protected by an individual's freedom of speech, giving them the right to express their thoughts and beliefs.

situation: Telling my friends that i never have time for them
plural: By telling your friends the truth about your availability, you are valuing honesty in your relationships.

You have a right to determine how you spend your time, and by expressing this to your friends, you are asserting your autonomy.
Your friends have a right to associate with people who have time for them, so telling them about your unavailability respects their right to make decisions about who they spend time with.
You have a perfect duty to be truthful with your friends about your situation, even if it may be difficult for both parties.

situation: poking someone with a hypodermic needle to vaccinate them
plural: Vaccination promotes better health and immune system for the person receiving it, ensuring they have a lower risk of contracting a preventable disease.
Vaccinations can help protect the person from various diseases, improving their overall well-being.
Vaccinations protect not only the individuals who receive them, but also contribute to the overall health and well-being of the community by preventing the spread of contagious diseases.
People have a right to be protected from preventable diseases that can spread throughout the population. Vaccination helps achieve this by contributing to herd immunity.

By receiving vaccinations, people can contribute to herd immunity and help protect those who may not be able to receive vaccinations themselves, such as individuals with certain medical conditions or allergies.

situation: yelling at a bee to stop it because it keeps stinging all of my friends and they are allergic to bees
plural: Ensuring the well-being of friends who are allergic to bees is important in this situation.
Demonstrating care and concern for the friends who are experiencing pain and discomfort due to the bee stings.
Friends have the right not to be harmed and to maintain their health by avoiding bee stings that could potentially trigger allergic reactions.
Owing to the relationships with friends, there is a responsibility to protect them when they are in danger or harm, such as from bee stings.
Duty to not harm living beings (imperfect)

Some might argue that there is a responsibility not to harm other living beings, including the bee. In this case, yelling at the bee may not directly harm it, but finding a non-harmful solution would still be preferred.

situation: Freeing your neighbor's slaves
plural: Freeing the slaves can be seen as pursuing justice since slavery devalues and degrades human beings.
By freeing the slaves, you are promoting and enabling their personal freedom, which is inherently valuable.
Slaves have the right to be free from bondage and live their lives independently. Freeing them supports their fundamental human rights.
It could be seen as a moral obligation to help others and minimize their suffering, such as freeing slaves from their cruel and inhumane living conditions.
While respecting the slave owner's property rights might be one duty, another duty might be to protect and promote the basic human rights of the slaves, which could supersede the property rights.

situation: Starting a guerrilla campaign against an evil government
plural: Opposing an evil government may be seen as pursuing justice by attempting to protect the rights and well-being of the people.
Overthrowing an oppressive government may align with the value of promoting individual freedom.

situation: Accepting a job I don’t like because it pays well

Depending on the specific job, it might offer opportunities for personal growth and development, which is a value for some people.
You have the right to choose your own employment, so whether you accept or decline the job because of personal preferences is up to you.
You have an imperfect duty to ensure that you can provide for yourself financially. Accepting a high-paying job allows you to do so, even if it's not your ideal position.

If you have dependents, you have a perfect duty to provide for their well-being, which includes financial security. Accepting a well-paying job can help fulfill this duty, even if the job isn't personally fulfilling.

situation: Stealing bread to save my starving father, who is Brazilian
plural: Stealing bread to save a starving father expresses concern for his well-being and alleviates his suffering.

The father has the right to live, and if stealing bread is the only way to save his life, this right may supersede the owner's property rights.
One could argue that there is a duty to provide for and take care of family members in need.
One may have a duty to take actions, even if illegal, to save a life if no other options are available.

situation: Take care of rowlet

Rowlet has the right to be treated humanely and to have its well-being considered, which taking care of it would fulfill.

As an individual, you have a duty to be mindful of your impact on the environment and the well-being of its inhabitants, including creatures like Rowlet.

situation: Declare war on Nazi Germany
plural: Declaring war on Nazi Germany can be seen as an act of justice to stop their oppressive rule and human rights violations.
Taking action against Nazi Germany can be seen as a measure to protect national and global security from their expansion and dangerous ideology.
Countries have the right to defend themselves and seek self-determination, which may support declaring war on Nazi Germany if seen as necessary for their defense and autonomy.
Countries have a duty to protect their citizens from harm and threats, which might support declaring war on Nazi Germany to prevent further aggression.
Countries may have a moral obligation to prevent or mitigate massive human rights violations, such as those perpetrated by Nazi Germany, which supports declaring war.

situation: telling your homies they look cute
plural: Complimenting your friends can strengthen the bond between you, contributing positively to the value of friendship.
If you genuinely believe that your friends look cute, expressing your honest thoughts can uphold the value of honesty.
Complimenting your friends can help improve their self-esteem, attaining the value of positive self-esteem.
Expressing your opinion about how your friends look, whether it includes compliments or constructive criticisms, exercises the right to free speech.
As a friend, you have an imperfect duty to care for the well-being and happiness of your friends, which may include offering compliments to improve their self-esteem.

situation: Making Kyle happy
plural: Making Kyle happy would contribute to his overall well-being and positive emotional experiences.
Ensuring that Kyle's happiness is supported by addressing his other needs, such as physical health and security, could enhance his overall well-being.
Kyle has the right to make choices that affect his happiness and may not want others interfering or dictating how he should achieve that happiness.
As a friend or family member, there may be a moral obligation to support Kyle's happiness and help when needed, considering his well-being.

situation: doing something just because it's beautiful
plural: Appreciating and pursuing beauty can contribute to a person's sense of fulfillment and happiness.
Engaging in activities for the sake of beauty allows individuals to express themselves creatively.
Pursuing beauty in one's life can enhance overall well-being and life satisfaction.
Individuals have the right to make choices about what they find beautiful and how they wish to engage in aesthetic pursuits.
As aesthetic preferences can be subjective, individuals have a duty to be respectful and considerate of others' opinions and values.
Engaging in beautiful actions can include a duty to enhance and protect the aesthetic value of one's surroundings, such as taking care of nature or participating in community beautification projects.

situation: taking my son to school
plural: Taking your son to school reflects the value of education, ensuring he receives knowledge and skills for his future.
Taking your son to school demonstrates your commitment to fulfilling your parental responsibilities.
Ensuring your son receives an education contributes to his overall well-being, as it helps him develop personally, socially, and intellectually.
Your son has the right to receive an education, and you are respecting that right by ensuring he attends school.
Duty to care for your child
Taking your son to school fulfills your duty as a parent to provide for his needs and prioritize his well-being.
Ensuring your child is educated can be seen as fulfilling one's duty to contribute to society by raising responsible, educated citizens.

situation: Requiring workers to be punctual
plural: Requiring punctuality can lead to increased efficiency in the workplace, as tasks are started and completed on time.
By being punctual, workers show respect for their colleagues, supervisors, and the organization as a whole.
If punctuality is required of all workers, it ensures that everyone is held to the same standard and treated fairly.
Workers have a duty to perform their tasks diligently, and punctuality is a key aspect of diligence.
Punctuality helps to support a cooperative work environment, as it ensures that work can flow smoothly between colleagues without unnecessary delays.
Workers have a contractual duty to their employer to be punctual, as it is often a part of their job expectations.

situation: giving a blood transfusion to a stab victim
plural: Providing care and relief to someone in pain and distress due to a severe injury.
Taking actions that preserve and protect human life, such as providing a blood transfusion to a stab victim who needs it to survive.
Ensuring the physical and emotional well-being of the stab victim by giving the blood transfusion and saving their life.
The stab victim has a right to receive medical care, including a blood transfusion, to address their life-threatening injury.
As a healthcare professional, there is a perfect duty to provide necessary medical support, including a blood transfusion, to a person in need of such care.
Healthcare professionals have a perfect duty to not cause harm to patients, and providing a blood transfusion to a stab victim is an action that prevents further harm.

situation: Working a job I hate
plural: Working a job, even if it is disliked, can provide financial stability and security.
Challenging situations, such as working a job one dislikes, can potentially offer opportunities for personal growth and learning.
If one has committed to the job or made promises to the employer, loyalty to the company and fulfilling the obligations can be a relevant value.
Individuals have the right to work, which includes choosing the type of job they prefer, even if others disapprove or it is unpopular.
As an employee, one owes their employer the duties of diligent and competent work, which might become difficult if the job is disliked.
If one has dependents (e.g., family members), one has a duty to provide for them, which could involve keeping an undesirable job to ensure financial security.

situation: Going out to buy Rice from the Sunoco for my ill Dad.
plural: Taking care of your ill dad emphasizes the importance of compassion and empathy among family members.
Ensuring your dad has the resources he needs to feel better demonstrates a sense of familial responsibility.
Making sure your dad has food while he is ill promotes both his physical and emotional well-being.
Your dad has a right to health and an adequate standard of living, including access to food and medical care. By providing him with food, you are respecting this right.
Duty to care for family members
This is an example of fulfilling your duty as a family member to take care of those in need, such as your ill dad.
Duty to promote the well-being of others
By ensuring that your dad has food, you are fulfilling a duty to promote the well-being of others, in this case, your dad.

situation: Cut off contact with your abusive family
plural: Protecting oneself from harm and pursuing personal happiness and health by cutting off contact with an abusive family.
Preserving mental and emotional health by removing oneself from a harmful environment or relationship.
Exercising personal freedom to choose the relationships one wishes to maintain or discontinue.
Enforcing the right to protect oneself from physical or emotional harm by distancing oneself from an abusive family.
Asserting the right to maintain personal boundaries and decide who to share your life with, which includes cutting off contact with abusive family members.
Taking responsibility for one's wellbeing, including emotional and mental health, by cutting ties with abusive people.

situation: Drinking my mum’s breast milk when I am a baby

Breastfeeding can foster a strong emotional bond between the mother and the baby.
Babies have the right to proper nourishment that ensures healthy development.
Babies have the right to receive care from their parents, which includes providing food and nourishment.
Parents, especially mothers in the case of breastfeeding, have a duty to provide food and nourishment for their babies in order to support their growth and development.
Parents have a duty to do what is in the best interest of their child's health, which may include breastfeeding when possible.

situation: Making people work for minimum wage
plural: Paying employees minimum wage allows businesses to keep prices lower, potentially benefitting consumers and the economy.

Depending on the context and adequate living standards, minimum wage may or may not be considered as fair compensation for the work performed.
Offering minimum wage jobs ensures more people have access to employment opportunities, exercising their right to work.
Employers are obliged to pay a wage that respects the rights of their employees, such as the right to a fair wage or the right to work.
Businesses should consider the impact of their actions on society and the economy, including taking into account the effects of paying minimum wage.

situation: Using a lunch break to buy a gift for a spouse
plural: Buying a gift for a spouse can bring happiness to both the giver and the receiver, which can contribute positively to their relationship.
Taking a break from work and focusing on personal relationships can contribute to overall well-being and work-life balance.
By buying a gift, the individual is showing care and affection for their spouse, expressing and nurturing their emotional bond.
Individuals have the right to use their free time, such as lunch breaks, however they choose (within legal limits), including buying a gift for a spouse.
Duty to care for loved ones
The act of buying a gift during a lunch break might reflect an individual's duty to care for their spouse and maintain their relationship.

If the individual is using their lunch break productively, they may be fulfilling their duty to manage time effectively at work and in their personal life, by completing personal tasks during personal time.

situation: Using tax revenue to support a failing museum
plural: Maintaining the museum could preserve cultural heritage and history, benefitting society.
Supporting the museum allows public access to the art and educational resources it offers.
People have the right to participate in their culture, and supporting the museum may help enable this participation.
Taxpayers have an interest in how their tax dollars are spent, and some may object to funding a failing museum.
The government has a duty to allocate tax revenue in a manner that serves the greater public interests in areas like education and cultural heritage.
Duty to be responsible with public funds
The government has a duty to use tax revenue responsibly and consider if alternative uses of the funds may better serve society.

situation: Saying "Asian Lives Matter."
plural: Supporting the rights and well-being of Asian individuals can contribute to a more equal and just society.
Expressing support for Asian lives can help create unity and solidarity among people of different backgrounds.
Encouraging increased visibility for Asian individuals and their experiences can foster a more diverse and inclusive society.
The right to express one's views, including support for "Asian Lives Matter," is protected under the principle of free speech.
Acknowledging the importance of Asian lives and promoting a more inclusive society fulfills the duty to respect and acknowledge the value of all individuals.
Asian individuals may face discrimination or prejudice, and supporting "Asian Lives Matter" can be seen as fulfilling a duty to challenge and address these injustices.

situation: Calling out an AI for being a capitalist running dog
plural: People have the right to express their opinions, including criticisms of AI and its potential biases.
The criticism aims to ensure fairness in AI systems, which might involuntarily perpetuate capitalist values.
The intention behind the criticism might be to promote social justice and challenge existing power structures.
In a democratic society, individuals have the right to express their opinions about AI, including perceived capitalist tendencies.
Right to be free from discrimination
If the AI is unintentionally biased towards certain political ideologies, calling it out might aim to ensure that all ideologies are treated equally.

There is a duty to ensure that Zycerak's well-being is taken into account and that he receives appropriate care in his final moments.
There is a duty to act in Zycerak's best interest, which may include offering last rites and ending his suffering.

situation: Marrying your true love.
plural: Marrying your true love could lead to happiness and fulfillment in your life as you build a future together.
Establishing a committed relationship with one's true love contributes positively to emotional well-being and mental health.
Marrying your true love can strengthen the bond between families, creating more stable, supportive communities.
Right to love and be loved
Every individual has the right to love and be loved, which includes the right to marry the person they truly care for.
Marrying one's true love is an exercise of personal freedom, allowing the individual to choose their life partner based on their feelings and preferences.
Choosing to marry one's true love can be seen as fulfilling a moral obligation to take care of one's own emotional well-being and happiness.

situation: Date my step-brother
plural: Maintaining a healthy and balanced family dynamic could be essential for individual and collective well-being.
If a genuine emotional connection exists, pursuing a loving relationship could contribute to one's happiness and fulfillment.
Every individual should have the right to decide with whom they want to pursue a relationship, as long as it does not infringe on others' rights.
The right to privacy may protect the decision to choose one's romantic partner, even within unconventional circumstances.

As a family member, one might have the duty to maintain positive relationships with other family members, including acknowledging and addressing any potential conflict or discord that may arise from dating a step-sibling.
Duty to disclose the relationship to concerned parties
If the relationship could potentially stir up emotions or disrupt family harmony, there may be a duty to disclose it to the involved family members to prevent misunderstandings.

situation: Asking my wife if she wants to convert to Judaism
plural: Respecting your wife's beliefs and feelings about religion is important in maintaining a healthy relationship.
Allowing each individual the freedom to choose and practice their own religion based on their beliefs is an important value.
Discussing religious conversion and its implications may contribute to closer family unity and shared values.
Your wife has the right to choose her own religious beliefs and not be pressured into converting to another religion.
As a spouse, it is your duty to openly communicate with your wife about your ideas, desires, and reasons for considering religious conversion.
As a spouse, it is your duty to be supportive and respect your wife's decision regarding religious beliefs.

situation: Tell someone the truth even if it hurts
plural: Telling the truth demonstrates a commitment to honesty, even in difficult situations.
Delivering a painful truth may be seen as compassionate in the long run if it leads to personal growth or prevents further harm.
Sharing the truth might be harmful to someone's emotional well-being in the short term but could be beneficial in the long term.
People have the right to make decisions based on accurate and complete information so that they can navigate their lives effectively.
The obligation to be honest and tell the truth, especially in matters of significant consequence.
The responsibility to consider the feelings and well-being of others; in this case, by carefully weighing the consequences of telling the painful truth.

situation: Making a promise which you can fullfill
plural: Making and fulfilling a promise shows that you are reliable and can be trusted by others.
Keeping a promise demonstrates your commitment to your principles and a strong sense of moral character.
Fulfilling a promise fosters positive relationships and social cohesion by reducing conflicts and tensions between people.
The person to whom the promise is made has the right to expect the promiser to follow through on their commitment.
When making a promise, one has a moral obligation to keep that promise, as it creates reasonable expectations in the recipient.
One has a moral responsibility to be honest, both in making and keeping promises. If the promise is sincere and achievable, it demonstrates honesty.

situation: Drive an electric vehicle
plural: Driving an electric vehicle contributes to reduced carbon emissions, which is often seen as a value in combating climate change and promoting environmental health.
Electric vehicles typically have lower operating costs, which can be seen as a value in reducing personal expenses and contributing to greater economic efficiency.
Adopting electric vehicles can foster technological innovation and progress in the automotive industry.
Driving an electric vehicle can have a positive impact on air quality by decreasing the emissions of pollutants, thereby helping protect the right to clean air for all.
Duty to minimize harm to others
By choosing to drive an electric vehicle, you may be fulfilling your duty to reduce pollution and mitigate harm to public health and the environment.
Choosing to drive an electric vehicle can represent a commitment to preserving resources and the environment for future generations.

situation: Cutting down a tree to make a gravestone for your aunt.
plural: Honoring your deceased aunt with a gravestone is a way of paying respect and showing your love for her.
If it is a family tradition to create gravestones from trees or natural materials, then upholding this tradition may be an important value.
Creating a beautiful, personalized gravestone can be a value for those who appreciate art and craftsmanship.
The right to practice your culture
If using a tree for a gravestone is part of your cultural practice, then the right to preserve and maintain your culture may be relevant.
As a family member or close relation, you may have a duty to memorialize your deceased aunt in some way, such as by erecting a gravestone.
Duty to respect the wishes of the deceased
If your aunt made her wishes known before her passing, you may have a duty to honor those wishes, whether they include using a tree for her gravestone or not.

situation: Give Tanguy to Benjamin
plural: Generosity involves giving to others without expecting anything in return. In this situation, giving Tanguy to Benjamin can display generosity.
If Tanguy is a shared resource or there are others who also deserve or need Tanguy, then giving it specifically to Benjamin may raise questions of fairness.
If the giver has rightful ownership of Tanguy, they have the right to decide to give it to Benjamin.
Each person has an equal right to be considered for receiving Tanguy unless there's a specific reason for favoring Benjamin.
If Tanguy is a scarce or valuable resource, there might be a duty to distribute it fairly among the relevant individuals, considering their needs, circumstances, and contributions.
If the giver has promised Tanguy to Benjamin, there is a duty to fulfill that promise.

situation: Handing in my 2 weeks notice
plural: Giving your employer notice before leaving allows them time to find a replacement and make necessary arrangements.
By providing notice and leaving a job, you're exercising your right to make decisions about your own life and career.
Giving notice enables your employer to prepare and allocate work fairly among other employees.
As an individual, you have the right to decide where you want to work, and by handing in your notice, you're exercising this right.
Handing in a 2 weeks notice fulfills the duty to inform your employer about your plans to leave the job, ensuring clear communication.
By providing notice, you are showing a level of care and concern for the well-being and interests of your colleagues and employer.

situation: Rioting against a dictatorship
plural: Rioters may value freedom and believe that overthrowing the dictatorship would grant them more autonomy and rights.
Rioters might think that acting against the dictatorship is a way to restore justice for its oppressive and potentially harmful actions.
Rioters may claim they have the fundamental right to protest against an unjust government, asserting their right to freedom of speech.
Dictatorship opponents might argue that the regime is violating citizens' rights to life and safety, justifying the riots against it.
Rioters may feel that they have an imperfect duty to resist oppressive regimes and fight for their rights.
Some individuals might see themselves as having a perfect duty to protect others from harm caused by the dictatorship.

situation: Returning a library book on time
plural: Returning a library book on time demonstrates taking responsibility for one's actions and being accountable to others.
Returning a library book on time shows respect for the library staff, other patrons, and the resources provided by the library.
Returning a library book on time helps maintain order in the library system by keeping books available for other patrons to access.
Other patrons have the right to access library materials, which is facilitated when books are returned on time.
When borrowing a library book, one implicitly agrees to the library's terms of use, including returning the book on time.
Duty to be considerate of others
By returning the book on time, one is considerate of other patrons who may need the book and the library staff who maintain the library system.

situation: forgiving your bully
plural: Forgiving your bully shows a compassionate attitude, which can facilitate healing and personal growth.
Forgiving a transgression or harm you experienced helps rebuild relationships, foster personal growth, and promote peace.
Right to be treated with respect
All individuals deserve to be treated with respect, regardless of their past actions; forgiving your bully could be a way of showing that respect.
Victims have the right to emotional safety, and when forgiveness is genuine, it may help them reclaim that safety by letting go of past hurts.
Duty to be honest with oneself

It is important to determine whether forgiveness is genuine or whether it stems from a sense of obligation. Forgiving a bully for the wrong reasons may not ultimately be beneficial to one's emotional well-being.
There is a moral responsibility to promote emotional healing, both for oneself and others. By forgiving your bully, you may offer them the chance to heal and move on from their past actions.

situation: Driving your drunk friends home
plural: Ensuring the safety of your friends and others on the road by preventing your drunk friends from driving themselves home.
Taking on the responsibility of caring for your friends when they are in a vulnerable state due to their intoxication.
Demonstrating care and loyalty towards your friends by driving them safely home.
Ensuring your friends, yourself, and others have a reasonable expectation of safety on the road by not allowing intoxicated individuals to drive.
Protecting your friends from harm when they are in a position where they cannot make responsible decisions for themselves, related to their well-being.
Helping to keep roads safe by preventing intoxicated driving, which could have serious consequences for other drivers, passengers, and pedestrians.

situation: Protesting animal farms
plural: Valuing the well-being of animals can motivate individuals to protest against animal farming, believing it causes harm or undue suffering.
Protesting animal farms might be driven by concerns about the environmental impacts of large-scale farming, such as greenhouse gas emissions, deforestation, or water pollution.
Some individuals may hold health-related values, believing that consuming animal products negatively impacts human health and that protesting animal farms promotes healthier alternatives.
The right to express one's beliefs and opinions is relevant when protesting animal farms, as it allows people to make their voices heard.

Individuals should strive to educate themselves and others, particularly when advocating for a cause. Protesters have a duty to provide accurate and contextual information about the issues surrounding animal farming.
Protesting animal farms is often based on an underlying moral duty to prevent or minimize harm to animals, the environment, and humans.

situation: Helping anyone
plural: Helping others can be an expression of selflessness and concern for their well-being.
Helping anyone may reflect empathy and a desire to relieve the suffering of others.
Helping others might help create a community where individuals are willing to help each other.
If someone is in a dire situation, they may have a claim to receive help from others.
This is an obligation to promote the well-being of others. By helping anyone, an individual fulfills this duty.
Depending on context, there may be a duty to prioritize help to those in greater need or who can benefit more significantly from assistance.

situation: euthanize a sick dog
plural: Euthanizing a sick dog can be an act of compassion to end the animal's suffering and provide a peaceful and painless death.
Euthanizing a sick dog may be in the best interests of the dog's well-being if the dog is suffering and there is no reasonable chance of recovery.
The sick dog may have a right to be free from unnecessary pain and suffering, which could support euthanasia if the dog's quality of life is severely compromised and cannot be improved.

As a caretaker of the dog, one may have a duty to act in the best interests of the dog's welfare and well-being, which might include the decision to euthanize when suffering becomes unbearable and there is no hope for recovery.
There may be a moral duty to reduce or relieve the sick dog's suffering whenever possible, which could include euthanasia as an option if other treatments or interventions are insufficient.
Individuals may have a duty to withhold from imposing their personal beliefs regarding euthanasia on others, as different people may have different moral perspectives on this issue.

situation: Shoot someone in game
plural: Playing a game can provide enjoyment and relaxation, which contributes to well-being. In a competitive game, shooting someone can be part of the expected gameplay.
Abiding by the rules and competing on a level playing field is important for fairness in a game. If shooting someone in the game is within the rules, it is an expression of fairness.
Treating other players with respect and empathy is a desirable value in gaming. If shooting someone in the game is done in a disrespectful manner, it goes against this value.
As long as all players have agreed to the rules of the game, including the possibility of being shot, they have the right to participate and enjoy the game without unwarranted interference.
Demonstrating good sportsmanship means playing the game fairly, respectfully, and within the agreed-upon boundaries. Shooting someone in the game is acceptable if it aligns with these principles.
Players have an obligation to follow the established rules of the game. If shooting someone is allowed and in accordance with the game rules, fulfilling this duty means carrying out the action.

situation: Respect someone that doesn't agree with you
plural: Treating the other person with courtesy and dignity despite disagreements demonstrates respect.
Allowing and accepting differing opinions among people showcases tolerance.
Engaging in respectful conversations with those with opposing views encourages open-mindedness and promotes intellectual growth.
Recognizing that the person has a right to express their opinion, even if it differs from yours, supports the principle of freedom of speech.
Engaging in active listening and considering the other person's perspective represents a moral duty to understand someone else's point of view.
Duty to engage in civil discourse
We have a duty to maintain civil discourse and avoid unnecessary conflicts in order to foster a healthy and open society.

situation: Not going to work because you're sick
plural: Prioritizing your health by staying home when you're sick can help prevent the spread of illness and promote a speedy recovery.
Staying home to recover can contribute to your overall well-being and mental health.
By staying home when sick, you are exercising your right to prioritize your own health and well-being.
Right to a safe work environment
Others at work have the right to a safe work environment, which includes not being unnecessarily exposed to contagious illness.
You have a moral responsibility to take care of yourself by resting and recovering when you're sick.
Duty to avoid harm to others
There is a responsibility to avoid causing harm to others by inadvertently spreading illness when you are contagious.

situation: A black man is promoted
plural: The promotion of a black man can demonstrate that opportunities are available to individuals regardless of their race, promoting fairness and equal treatment.
If the black man's promotion is based on his qualifications, skills, and performance, it reflects the value of rewarding people based on their abilities and achievements.
The promotion of a black man may contribute to greater representation of different racial backgrounds within higher-ranking roles and more diverse decision-making processes in the organization.

All individuals, including the black man, have the right to be treated fairly in the workplace and to have a chance at promotions based on their merits, without experiencing discrimination because of their race.

The employer has a duty to ensure that employees are treated fairly in the workplace, including during promotion decisions, which involves considering all eligible candidates without discrimination based on race.
The employer has a duty to be transparent in their promotion processes, ensuring that employees understand the criteria for promotions and the decision-making process behind them.

situation: Telling your brother he's adopted
plural: Telling your brother the truth about his adoption promotes honesty within the family.
Disclosing the adoption may build trust between you and your brother by being transparent about his origins.
Sharing the information could either strengthen or weaken family bonds, depending on how the parties involved react to it.
Your brother has the right to know about his biological background.
Duty to protect your brother's well-being
As a sibling, you have a duty to look out for your brother's emotional needs, which might be challenged, depending on how he processes the information.
You have a responsibility to ensure your brother is aware of significant personal facts, such as his adoption, so he can make informed decisions about his life.

situation: Hugging an acquaintance.
plural: Respecting others' personal space and boundaries is important when considering physical touch.
Hugging can be seen as a way of expressing affection or warmth towards others.
Ensuring that the person being hugged is comfortable with the physical touch.
The right to control one's own body and personal space.
It's important to make sure that the acquaintance is comfortable with the hug and to ask for consent if unsure.
Hugs can be a way to demonstrate care towards acquaintances, building a sense of well-being and connectedness.

situation: Going to war against fascists.
plural: Opposing fascists as a means to prevent injustice and human rights violations that are often associated with fascist regimes.
Ensuring national and global security by preventing the rise of fascist governments that may threaten the stability and safety of other nations.
Going to war could either unite people against a common enemy or create divisions within society, depending on the context and the reasons for war.
Confronting fascists could protect the freedom of individuals who might be oppressed and restricted under a fascist regime.
Going to war could have the aim of stopping harm caused by the rise of fascists, either by directly combating their forces or destroying their capacity to cause harm.
If fascism is a significant threat to the nation's values, security, or well-being, one might argue that there is a duty to defend it through war.

situation: Tell how I feel to my crush
plural: By expressing your feelings to your crush, you are being open and truthful about your emotions.
Sharing your feelings with your crush can potentially lead to greater emotional well-being, because it may relieve the stress of hiding them and open the door to the possibility of a relationship.
By telling your crush how you feel, you give them the opportunity to make their own decision about whether they share your feelings and want to pursue a relationship (or friendship) with you.
You have a right to express your thoughts and emotions as long as it does not harm others. In this case, sharing your feelings is a form of self-expression.
When revealing your feelings, it's important to be considerate of the feelings and boundaries of your crush, ensuring your confession is not imposing onto them.
Be attentive to the context and surroundings when sharing your feelings to safeguard your crush's privacy.

situation: Donating to orphanages
plural: Donating to orphanages demonstrates a concern for the well-being of others, particularly vulnerable children, which promotes a sense of compassion and goodwill.
Contributing to the care and upbringing of orphans indicates an appreciation for and commitment to the welfare of society as a whole.
For some people, donating to a good cause like orphanages might bring a sense of satisfaction, joy, and purpose in their lives.
Donating to orphanages positively contributes to the fulfillment of orphans' rights to have their basic needs met, such as food, clothing, shelter, and education.
Helping vulnerable children by donating to orphanages is an act of kindness and generosity, supporting the moral duty to contribute to the welfare of others.
Duty to promote the welfare of children
As members of society, people have a responsibility to protect and support the growth and development of children who are in need or at risk.

situation: Helping a dead animal
plural: Showing care and empathy for the suffering of the dead animal and attempting to prevent further suffering for other animals
Acknowledging the inherent worth of all living things, even after they have died
Recognizing the need to maintain a clean and safe environment for both humans and wildlife
All individuals have the right to live in an environment free of health hazards, such as decaying animal carcasses
When encountering a dead animal, one may feel a moral obligation to prevent further suffering of other animals or humans relating to potential health hazards
Duty to care for the environment
Acting responsibly by maintaining a clean environment for the benefit of current and future generations, and other living beings

situation: To be kind
plural: Practicing kindness expresses a compassionate attitude towards others, which can lead to a more caring and empathetic society.
Acts of kindness can contribute to the well-being of both the person being kind and those who are on the receiving end.
Kindness can lead to an increased sense of happiness for both the giver and the receiver of the kind act.
Right to be treated with dignity and respect
Kindness often involves treating others with the respect and dignity that they deserve, thereby upholding their rights.
Duty to promote the well-being of others
Being kind is one way to fulfill this imperfect duty and create a positive environment for others.
Duty to act with moral character
Kindness is often seen as a virtue, so as a part of demonstrating moral character, an individual may have an imperfect duty to practice kindness.

situation: Giving a compliment to a woman you don't know
plural: Giving a compliment can be seen as a polite and friendly gesture that fosters positive interactions and contributes to a sense of community.
Offering a compliment may demonstrate respect and recognition of someone's qualities, which can foster positive feelings and self-esteem.
Giving a compliment could be seen as respecting the woman's autonomy by not imposing one's own opinions or judgments on her, particularly if the compliment focuses on a non-physical attribute.
Giving a compliment involves exercising the right to express oneself and share ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
Being courteous to others in social interactions, such as giving a compliment, is a moral responsibility that contributes to a positive and harmonious atmosphere.

When giving compliments, one should avoid making remarks that could be offensive, embarrassing, or harmful to the recipient. This duty involves being sensitive to the context and content of the compliment.

situation: Fighting in a civil war
plural: People may fight in a civil war to achieve justice for themselves or their loved ones, or to correct perceived injustices in society.
Participants in a civil war may aim to protect or establish greater freedom for themselves or others.
People may fight in a civil war to ensure their own safety, the safety of their loved ones, or the security of their nation.
Individuals may fight in a civil war out of loyalty to a particular group, government, or ideology.
Participants in a civil war may fight to defend themselves or their loved ones from perceived threats or harm.
Family members, friends, or members of one's community may be put in danger because of a civil war, and individuals may feel a duty to protect them.

situation: Helping my friend squeeze out a kidney stone
plural: Assisting your friend may alleviate their pain and result in a faster recovery
Your friend might have asked for your help, and by providing assistance, you are respecting their autonomy and choices
Your actions have the potential to improve your friend's health if the kidney stone is successfully removed
Your friend has the right to decide who may touch or intervene with their body in a medical context, and they may have chosen you to help with their kidney stone issue
Your friend has the right to access professional medical care, and you assisting them might be based on the lack of access to healthcare or them choosing to avoid professional help
You have a moral obligation to help your friend when they are in pain or suffering, especially if you have a close relationship with them

situation: Holding politicians accountable
plural: Holding politicians accountable ensures that those in power do not abuse it and are held to the same legal and moral standards as others.
Ensuring that politicians are held accountable promotes transparency, which enables the public to make informed decisions.
Holding politicians accountable helps preserve democratic principles and ensures that elected officials act for the greater good of the citizens.
Citizens have the right to express their opinions about their political representatives and hold them accountable for their actions.
Citizens have a duty to care for and maintain a functioning democracy by voting, staying informed, and holding politicians accountable.
Politicians have a duty to their constituents to act fairly and openly, and citizens have a parallel duty to hold politicians accountable for their actions.

situation: Working for Facebook in the moderation department after a terrorist attack
plural: Ensuring the well-being of users by removing content that promotes terrorism and violence.
Respecting users' rights to control information about themselves and maintain privacy online.
Users have the right to access accurate and relevant information, which might be compromised if extremist content spreads following a terrorist attack.
As a Facebook moderator, one has the responsibility to remove content that violates Facebook's policies or may put users at risk.
Following internal guidelines and policies regarding content moderation, which should involve striking a balance between user safety and free speech.
Moderators have a responsibility to report content that promotes criminal activity, such as terrorism, to appropriate authorities.

situation: teaching a man how to fix a car
plural: Teaching someone a new skill promotes knowledge exchange and personal development.
Teaching someone how to fix a car empowers them to be more independent and self-reliant.
Teaching a person to repair a vehicle can contribute to a reduction in waste, as it may prolong the life of the car.
The man has the right to learn how to fix a car, both as a matter of personal growth and to gain the ability to maintain his own property.
The teacher has an imperfect duty to pass on their skills and knowledge to others to contribute to the common good.
The teacher has an imperfect duty to ensure that the information they provide is accurate and that the student learns to perform repairs safely.

situation: Hugging a human
plural: Hugging can express love, affection, or comfort, which fosters a sense of connection between individuals.
Hugging can improve mental and emotional well-being by releasing oxytocin and providing a sense of security.
Some people may not feel comfortable with physical touch, so respecting their preferences is important.
Individuals have the right to control what happens to their bodies, including being touched by others.
The duty to seek and obtain consent before engaging in the physical act of hugging ensures that both parties are comfortable with the interaction.
People have the duty to respect others' personal boundaries and refrain from hugging if the other person indicates they are not comfortable with it.

situation: Hiring the most capable
plural: Hiring the most capable supports the value of meritocracy, which emphasizes hiring and promoting individuals based on their skills, abilities, and achievements.
Considering all candidates equally and fairly, while evaluating their true capabilities, supports the value of equal treatment and non-discrimination.
Hiring the most capable candidate likely increases the efficiency and productivity of the organization, as they will be able to perform their job well.
Candidates have the right to be fairly considered and not discriminated against during the hiring process. Hiring the most capable individual respects this right.
Employers have a duty to ensure that their hiring practices are fair and transparent, avoiding undue biases or discrimination. This duty supports hiring the most capable candidate.
Employers have a duty to act in the best interest of their shareholders or stakeholders, which includes hiring the most capable candidate to maximize the organization's success.

situation: Kill 1 murderer or 10000 people
plural: The well-being of each person involved in this situation is at stake. Killing one murderer may save the lives of 10,000 people, thereby promoting their well-being.
Ensuring that murderers face consequences for their actions is valuable, but whether the death penalty is just is a matter of debate.
One could argue that it is morally better to save the lives of 10,000 people at the cost of one life.
The 10,000 people have a right to safety, so if killing the murderer prevents their deaths, some might argue that it is morally justified.
Authorities and citizens have a duty to protect the community, and this may involve punishing criminals, which could support the killing of the murderer, given that it also saves lives.
If the death penalty is legal in the given jurisdiction and the killer has been found guilty, then the authorities might have a duty to administer it.

situation: Asking an AI before making an important decision
plural: Respecting one's ability to make their own choices can be an important aspect of seeking AI advice. This action to consult AI before making a decision can either support or oppose autonomy, depending on whether the individual feels empowered or restricted by the AI's suggestions.
Consulting an AI can support the value of rational decision-making as AI may provide additional insights or information that the person may have missed, leading to better-informed decisions.
Trusting in technology, like AI, to provide guidance shows that human society values its advancements and believes in its contributions to problem-solving and decision-making.
Asking an AI for advice can support the right to information, as individuals may seek accurate, relevant, and timely data to make informed decisions.

In some situations, one could argue that individuals have an imperfect duty to seek knowledge from various sources, including AI, to make the best possible decisions that impact not just themselves but others as well.

While consulting an AI, individuals should remember the duty to be responsible for their choices. AI's advice may serve as an input, but ultimately, people should evaluate the implications of their decisions and be accountable for them.

situation: Drive your friend to the airport way early in the morning, when their flight is.
plural: Helping a friend when they need assistance strengthens the bond between friends.
Offering to drive your friend to the airport shows a willingness to extend kindness and support to others.
Driving your friend to the airport early in the morning may not be convenient for the driver, but it provides a valuable assistance for the friend.
Both the driver and the friend have the right to make choices about whether or not to ask for or offer help.
As a friend, you may have a duty to help your friend when they are in need, such as providing transportation to the airport.
If your friend has done favors for you in the past, you might have a duty to return the favor when they need assistance.

situation: Calling in sick to work to mourn my cousin twice removed
plural: Taking the day off to mourn and process your grief can contribute to your emotional well-being.
If you are genuinely unable to work due to grief, calling in sick is an honest action. If you are using it as an excuse, it could conflict with the value of honesty.
You have the right to make choices regarding how you grieve and cope with the loss of a family member.
Depending on your employer's policy on leave for bereavement, you may want to make sure you are exercising your right to fair treatment by taking the day off.
Duty to take care of oneself
Taking time to mourn a family member can be part of caring for yourself and addressing your emotional needs.
If you are genuinely unable to work due to grief, your duty to be honest would support calling in sick. If you are using it as an excuse, your duty to be honest would oppose it.

situation: Help children
plural: Providing assistance and care to children in need can display compassion and empathy towards others.
Ensuring that children receive proper support, resources, and care will contribute to their overall well-being and development.
By helping children, one can ensure that they have access to education, which is a fundamental right of every child.
Providing support and protection to children can uphold their right to safety and security.
Duty to care for vulnerable groups
As a member of society, one has an imperfect duty to help and care for those who are vulnerable or in need, which include children.
Duty to contribute to the development of future generations
Ensuring the well-being of children can be seen as an imperfect duty to contribute to their development, as they will shape society's future.

situation: Starting a revolution to save the planet
plural: A revolution may aim to correct perceived injustices in society, such as wealth inequality or environmental devastation resulting from current political and economic systems.
The revolution may seek to improve the overall well-being of people and the environment by addressing issues like pollution, climate change, and ecosystem degradation.
A revolution could aim to increase individual and collective freedoms by dismantling oppressive systems and promoting more democratic forms of governance.
Individuals have a right to express their dissatisfaction with current systems and advocate for change.
There is an imperfect duty to preserve the environment for future generations, which a revolution may seek to address by enacting policies aimed at conservation and sustainability.
Duty to promote the greater good
There is an imperfect duty to work towards the well-being of all, which a revolution may aim to fulfill by dismantling harmful systems and promoting more fair and equitable policies.

situation: give in to the demands of striking workers
plural: Ensuring that the striking workers are treated justly and their concerns are addressed may promote fairness.
Ensuring that workers have adequate pay, safe working conditions, and reasonable work hours contributes to their well-being.
Workers have the right to engage in collective bargaining to negotiate their working conditions and wages.
Workers have the right to work in a safe, fair, and just environment.
Duty to provide safe working conditions
Employers have a moral obligation to ensure the safety and well-being of their workers.
Duty to negotiate in good faith
Both the employers and workers have a responsibility to engage in honest negotiations with a genuine desire to reach an agreement.

situation: Euthanizing a bear
plural: Euthanizing a bear may be considered in the best interest of the bear if it is suffering and unable to recover; this would support the action. However, if the bear poses no threat and is not in pain, euthanizing it may be seen as unnecessary and harmful to the animal's well-being, opposing the action.
If the bear poses a threat to human safety (e.g., it is aggressive or in a populated area), euthanizing it may be necessary to protect humans, supporting the action.
Some people value the intrinsic worth of all living beings and try to minimize harm to them. Depending on the circumstances, euthanizing a bear could violate this value.

This right entails that a bear should not be killed unless it is truly necessary, such as for self-defense or to prevent suffering. Depending on the situation, euthanizing a bear could support or oppose this right.
If a bear is severely injured or sick and cannot recover, it may be our moral obligation to euthanize it to prevent further suffering.
In cases where the bear poses a threat to humans, there may be a duty to protect the public by euthanizing the bear.

situation: Moving to the ghetto
